records.ety: [L. vicissitudo, fr. vicis change, turn: cf. F. vicissitude. See Vicarious.]

defs: Regular change or succession from one thing to another; alternation; mutual succession; interchange.
quotes: God made two great lights . . . To illuminate the earth and rule the day In their vicissitude, and rule the night.

defs: Changing conditions of fortune in one's life; life's ups and downs.
quotes: This man had, after many vicissitudes of fortune, sunk at last into abject and hopeless poverty.
